items.title: Bahrain Says Air Defenses Intercept Iranian Missiles and Drones
items.desc: Bahrain says its air defense systems intercepted Iranian missiles and drones while authorities urged public vigilance as regional military tensions continued to intensify.

items.title: Kurdistan Region and Uzbekistan Deepen Trade Ties with Strategic Business Deals
items.desc: Business leaders from both sides gathered in Tashkent to expand trade, investment, and long-term economic cooperation through a series of strategic agreements.

items.title: Russia Says More Than 370 Drones Targeted Moscow Region Overnight
items.desc: Russian officials say air defenses intercepted most of more than 370 drones launched toward the Moscow region as wider attacks struck logistics infrastructure and intensified security concerns.

items.title: Iraq, U.S. Sign 48 Agreements as Iraqi PM Advances Broad Economic Agenda
items.desc: The agreements span energy, technology, education, agriculture and industry, underscoring Baghdad's strategy to diversify its economy, strengthen infrastructure and attract long-term American investment.

items.title: Iran Reports Tanker Explosions in Strait of Hormuz as Regional Tensions Escalate
items.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said two oil tankers exploded in the Strait of Hormuz and announced military operations targeting US positions in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain.

items.title: Dollar edges up in Baghdad and Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad/ Erbil The US dollar opened Saturday’s trading higher in Iraq, hovering around 153,000 dinars per 100 dollars. According to Shafaq News market survey, the dollar traded in Baghdad’s Al-Kifah and Al-Harithiya exchanges at 153,250 dinars per 100 dollars, up from the previous session’s 153,000 dinars. items.title: Al-Diwaniyah land committee head among four arrested over fraud
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Diwaniyah Iraq’s Federal Commission of Integrity said on Saturday that four employees at the Al-Diwaniyah Municipality Directorate, including the head of a committee responsible for reviewing residential land applications, were arrested on allegations of deliberately causing financial harm to the state. items.title: Kuwait Airways halts flights after Iranian strikes
items.desc: Shafaq News- Kuwait Kuwait Airways temporarily suspended operations at Kuwait International Airport on Saturday following Iranian missile and drone attacks on the country, with most flights rescheduled. #kuwait_airways announces to its valued customers the rescheduling of most of its flights due to the temporary suspension of takeoff and landing operations at Kuwait International Airport as a resu

items.title: Basrah crudes jump 10%+ for the week
items.desc: Shafaq News- Basrah Iraq’s Basrah crude prices rose more than 12% over the past week, outperforming several major global oil benchmarks. Basrah Heavy crude fell by $1.94 in its latest trading session to $54.89 per barrel, a daily decline of 3.41%, but recorded weekly gains of $6.62, or 12.06%. items.title: Nippur: Iraq’s ancient city still revealing Mesopotamia’s secrets
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Diwaniyah More than 4,000 years after emerging as the spiritual heart of ancient Mesopotamia, the archaeological city of Nippur in Iraq’s Al-Diwaniyah province continues to yield discoveries that deepen understanding of the Sumerian, Akkadian, and Babylonian civilizations. items.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
items.desc: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.desc: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: He made history in Congress - then the Supreme Court changed its mind
items.desc: Residents say they're afraid that if Shomari Figures loses his seat, their federal-funding could disappear with him.

items.title: What to know about the Canadian and US wildfires and their impact
items.desc: Wildfires in Ontario have blanketed US and Canadian cities with dangerous smoke.

items.title: How the World Cup made an 'NFL-obsessed' American a football fan
items.desc: New fans tell the BBC they are fully invested after watching the World Cup on home soil, just don’t ask them to stop calling it soccer.

items.title: U.S. and Iran escalate strikes across Mideast
items.desc: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

items.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
items.desc: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.

items.title: The U.S.-Iran battle over the Strait of Hormuz raises risks for global waterways
items.desc: Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.

items.title: Pakistani forces kill 24 militants in border raids near Afghanistan
items.desc: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.

items.title: U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf
items.desc: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.

items.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
items.desc: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.

items.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
items.desc: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
items.desc: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
items.desc: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.
